fields.helpText: This allows users to select entries by the internal number.
This is the name of a patient that has some type of pharmacy order.
This field is used to indicate if safety caps are to be issued to patient.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:REGULAR MAIL;1:CERTIFIED MAIL;2:DO NOT MAIL;3:LOCAL - REGULAR MAIL;4:LOCAL - CERTIFIED MAIL;
fields.helpText: This field is used to: A) Determine whether this patient's Rx's are to be sent to the CMOP, or retained for local distribution. If 2-4 are selected, none of this patient's Rx's will be transmitted to the CMOP. B) Select what the mail priority is.  The CMOP choices are limited to (0) REGULAR and (1) CERTIFIED. Local mail may be designated (3) LOCAL - REGULAR or (4) LOCAL - CERTIFIED. The 'DO NOT MAIL' code (2) may be used to ensure that the patient's Rx's are not mailed.
This field is used to indicate if the patient is in dialysis.

This field places a time limit on the 'Do Not Mail', 'Local - Regular Mail' and 'Local - Certified Mail' conditions in the 'MAIL' field. If a date is placed in this field and the software detects that the date the Rx is processed is greater than the date in the field (past the expired date) a default value of 'Regular Mail' will be assumed for the 'MAIL' field.   NOTE: The actual value of the 'MAIL' field will not be changed by the software, but can only be modified by a user editing the 'MAIL'
This is the login date of the first order for pharmacy services.

The purpose of this field is to indicate whether the FIRST SERVICE DATE  field was populated with actual or historical data.  Actual indicates the  field was populated when the first pharmacy service was filed.   Historical indicates the field was populated by the conversion routine.
This is notable information about the patient that pertains to pharmacy.
This field is used to indicate a default patient status.
This field is used to indicate if the patient resides within a community nursing home.
This field is used to indicate if the patient has a nursing home contract.
This field is used to indicate the last date of the nursing home contract.
This field is used to indicate the start date of respite care.
This field is used to indicate the end date of respite care.
This field is used to indicate active prescriptions.
This sub-file is used to indicate prescription history for this patient.

This field is used to indicate if the patient's medications have been updated in the CPRS orders file. If the field is 0, nothing has been updated, 1 indicates the initial data movement is complete, and 2 indicates the Backfill to CPRS is complete.
This is the Non-VA Meds order multiple. It will be used to store the  Non-VA Med orders entered through the CPRS application.
This is the patient's authorization number assigned by national data base.
This is the patient's clozapine treatment status.
This is the date of the patient's last clozapine prescription.
This indicates whether patient demographic data has been sent to the national database.
This is the physician responsible for the patient's clozaril treatment.
This field is used to enter the date of registration.

This represents the 'top' of the UNIT DOSE SUB-FILE, where all of a patient's active (and expired & dc'd) UNIT DOSE orders are kept. Although orders are initially entered into ^PS(53.1), the order is transferred into this sub-file (^PSGOT) upon verification.

This is date used as a default value for the STOP DATE/TIME of Unit Dose orders during the order entry and renewal processes, if the corresponding site parameter is turned on.  The order entry and renewal processes will sometimes change this date.
This is the latest provider for this patient, used as a default value in order entry.
This is the last admission date for this patient.
This is the last transfer date for this patient.
This is the last discharge date for this patient.
This is a date used to update expired orders.  (Internal)

fields.setOfCodes: 0:ORDERS NOT ON HOLD;1:ORDERS ON HOLD;2:ORDERS AUTOMATICALLY PLACED ON HOLD;
fields.helpText: This is an internal flag set when all of the patient's orders have been placed on hold. If '2', all of a patient's Unit Dose AND IV orders were placed on hold automatically by the package.

This is an internal flag set when a patient is first found to have been discharged.  It is set so that Inpatient Meds does not try to cancel the patient's orders every time the patient is accessed while discharged.
This is used for temporary storage of data by the Unit Dose package. The data is deleted before and after each use!
This is an explanation of why this patient's orders are being placed on hold.  This explanation will show on the patient's Unit Dose profile.

This is the last Unit Dose order converted for use with version 4 of the Inpatient Medications package. THIS SHOULD NOT BE EDITED.  Editing could result in corrupted data and possibly medication administration errors.
This field stores the Inpatient pharmacy narrative for the patient.
This field is used by the UNIT DOSE PHARMACY package.  It is NOT to be entered into anywhere else!
This sub-file allows patient billing comments to be stored for this  patient.

This subfile contains IV order information for any given patient. A separate IV order file does not exist. This isn't the same design as  outpatient pharmacy -- which does contain a separate file for prescriptions.
This sub file is used to indicate archived prescriptions.

This field is updated by the IV PACKAGE every time action is taken on this particular patient.  This piece will have two pieces of information on it.  The first piece ('-' is the delimiter) is the current admissions number, and the second piece is the discharge date for this admissions number.  This field is used for determining whether or not to DC IV ORDERS due to ADMISSIONS or DISCHARGE.

This piece is updated by the IV PACKAGE every time this particular patient has any 'IV ACTION'.  This piece represents the service of the last ward that this patient was in.  It is a set of codes that was taken from the ADT package.  This field is used to determine whether or not to DC IV ORDERS due to SERVICE transfer.

This field is updated by the IV PACKAGE every time this particular patient has any 'IV ACTION'.  This field represents the last INTERWARD TRANSFER NUMBER for the LAST ADMISSION NUMBER.  This field is used to determine whether or not to DC IV ORDERS due to WARD TRANSFER. ORDERS due to SERVICE transfer.
This is the last IV order for a patient that had it's data converted to the New Person file (200).

This field contains a 1 if the conversion to the patient's orders for version 5.0 has  completed.  It contains a 2 if the conversion to the patient's orders for Pharmacy Ordering Enhancement (POE) has completed.   It contains a 3 if the patient's IV orders existed prior to BCMA V.2.0  has updated the Verifying Pharmacist field.
This is the unique barcode ID in the format DFN_"V"_ID# ex: 740V1.
This field is used to identify patients who have another language preference for printing medication instructions on bottle labels.
This field will be used to determine the default language preference for printing the PMI sheets at the CMOPs.
This multiple contains the history log information for ScripTalk  enrollment activities for a patient.
The CLINICAL ALERTS (multiple) allows Pharmacy staff to document clinical  information that is not covered anywhere else in the VistA packages,   e.g. when a patient is part of a research study.
